varieties.name: Palla Rossa (Chioggia)
varieties.origin: Chioggia, Veneto, Italy; developed early 20th century
varieties.traits: Dense round ball heads 10-15cm diameter, deep red/burgundy leaves with white veins, compact tight wrapping
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, reliable head formation, 55-60+ days to maturity depending on slot
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Eden Seeds, ActiveVista (organic), Quality Plants & Seedlings, Happy Valley Seeds

varieties.name: Rosso di Verona (Rossa di Verona)
varieties.origin: Verona, Veneto, Italy; emerged mid-late 18th century, commercialized early 19th century
varieties.traits: Football/oval shape 10-15cm, deep crimson-red leaves, thick crunchy white mid-ribs, triangular cup-shaped leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, needs cold weather for best quality, 70-85 days
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Weston Seeds, ActiveVista, The Heirloom Seed Store

varieties.name: Treviso Precoce (Early Treviso)
varieties.origin: Treviso, Veneto, Italy; cultivated since 16th century
varieties.traits: Elongated torpedo shape 25-35cm long, wine-red leaves, thick white mid-rib, dense football-shaped head
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, no forcing required unlike Tardivo, 70-100 days
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, specialty Italian seed suppliers, ActiveVista

varieties.name: Variegata di Castelfranco
varieties.origin: Castelfranco, Veneto, Italy; "The Tulip of Winter"
varieties.traits: Open tulip-like head, cream/butter-yellow leaves with red-purple variegation and speckles, elegant rosette form
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, requires cold for variegation development, 80-90 days

varieties.name: Rosa del Veneto (Pink Radicchio)
varieties.origin: Veneto, Italy; recent selection from Rosso di Verona, pink mutation
varieties.traits: Pink/rose colored heads (not red), rose-like layered appearance, 10-15cm, variable shape (football to open rosette)
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, less refined breeding, 70-85 days

varieties.name: Pan di Zucchero (Sugarloaf)
varieties.origin: Northern Italy; also known as Radicchio di Milano
varieties.traits: Large upright cylindrical heads like romaine/Napa cabbage cross, pale green, 30-40cm tall, tightly wrapped
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yielding, substantial heads, 70-90 days, excellent storage (3-4 months)
varieties.availability: Fedco Seeds, Seeds from Italy, The Heirloom Seed Store, Happy Valley Seeds

varieties.name: Brussels Witloof (Belgian Endive)
varieties.origin: Brussels, Belgium; discovered accidentally 1830s, developed by mid-1800s
varieties.traits: Two-stage crop: first grow roots 130-150 days, then force chicons in darkness 3-4 weeks. Roots 5cm diameter, 20-25cm long. Forced chicons 10-15cm pale yellow-white torpedo heads
varieties.yield_notes: One chicon per root, labor-intensive, premium product
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Johnny's Selected Seeds, Seeds from Italy, Victory Seed Company, The Heirloom Seed Store

varieties.name: Catalogna Puntarelle
varieties.origin: Lazio and Puglia regions, Italy; traditional Roman vegetable
varieties.traits: Elongated upright plant 40-50cm, white stems with serrated dark green dandelion-like leaves, hollow shoots
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, cut-and-come-again, 60-75 days
varieties.availability: Seeds from Italy, Happy Valley Seeds, The Seed Collection, ActiveVista (Catalogna Gigante di Chioggia)

varieties.name: Catalogna (Long Green/Long Stemmed)
varieties.origin: Italy; also known as Italian Dandelion, Radichetta
varieties.traits: Upright long stems, white ribs, green serrated leaves, 40-50cm tall, open habit
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, easy to grow, 60 days, multiple harvests

varieties.name: Grumolo Verde (Green)
varieties.origin: Italy; traditional cut-and-come-again variety
varieties.traits: Forms attractive rosette of intense green leaves, small bunches, compact heart at maturity
varieties.yield_notes: Good for salad mix, 30 days baby/50 days full size, rosettes form after cutting and winter dormancy
varieties.availability: Seeds of Italy, The Heirloom Seed Store, ActiveVista

varieties.name: Grumolo Rossa (Red)
varieties.origin: Italy; heritage variety
varieties.traits: Small voluminous heads, deep red concave leaves, compact heart, beautiful rosette formation
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, specialty item, 60-70 days

varieties.name: Magdeburg (Coffee Chicory)
varieties.origin: Magdeburg, Germany; developed for coffee substitute, European heritage
varieties.traits: Large thick taproot (primary harvest), bigger roots than salad varieties, 20-30cm long, 3-5cm diameter
varieties.yield_notes: Good root production, 100-120 days to mature roots, leaves also edible
varieties.availability: The Italian Gardener (Australia), midsummerherbs (Australia), Survival Garden Seeds, specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Chiavari Root Chicory
varieties.origin: Chiavari, Liguria, Italy; Slow Food Ark of Taste endangered variety
varieties.traits: Long cylindrical white root, smooth not stringy, conical shape, traditional scorzonera type
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, heritage preservation important, 100-120 days

varieties.name: Zuccherina di Trieste
varieties.origin: Trieste, Italy; cutting chicory
varieties.traits: Small tender leaves, resembles lettuce, sweet flavor for chicory, compact growth
varieties.yield_notes: Can be grown almost year-round in temperate zones except midsummer, 30-40 days

varieties.name: Tardivo di Treviso
varieties.origin: Treviso, Veneto, Italy; "King of Radicchio", 15th century origins, PGI protected
varieties.traits: Elongated burgundy leaves with thick white ribs, requires forcing/blanching in spring water, open curled appearance after forcing
varieties.yield_notes: Labor-intensive specialty crop, premium product, 90-120 days plus forcing
varieties.availability: Specialty Italian suppliers (for collectors only in Brisbane)

varieties.name: Catalogna (Long Green/Italian Dandelion)
varieties.origin: Italy; cutting chicory
varieties.traits: Upright stems 40-50cm, white ribs, serrated green leaves, open growth
varieties.yield_notes: Fast growing, 50-60 days for tender harvest, multiple cuts possible
varieties.availability: International seed suppliers, Happy Valley Seeds ships to India, online vendors

varieties.name: Pan di Zucchero (Sugarloaf)
varieties.origin: Northern Italy
varieties.traits: Large cylindrical heads, pale green, romaine-like, 30-40cm
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding if conditions suitable, 70-80 days

varieties.name: Root Chicory (for Coffee)
varieties.origin: European varieties, coffee cultivation tradition in India
varieties.traits: Deep taproot, grown for root harvest not leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Root production, 100-120 days
varieties.availability: Seeds available from northern Indian suppliers, but cultivation not recommended in Kerala lowlands

varieties.name: Palla Rossa (Chioggia)
varieties.origin: Chioggia, Italy
varieties.traits: Round red heads, needs cold for color
varieties.yield_notes: 55-70 days in ideal conditions

varieties.name: Zuccherina di Trieste
varieties.origin: Trieste, Italy
varieties.traits: Small tender leaves, sweet for chicory
varieties.yield_notes: Quick growing, 30-40 days
